metadata.sign_correction_source: DP24 (Stage 4 sign convention inversion, Track A2 detection)
metadata.stage8_dashboard_role: Forecast & Bridge tab — right panel chain visualization

nodes.label: CSI (Macro)
nodes.description: Consumer Sentiment Index, Bank of Korea ECOS, monthly forward-filled to weekly

nodes.label: NB Global Search
nodes.description: NB brand-level search demand in Global market (US/EU primary)

nodes.label: NB Korea Search
nodes.description: NB brand-level search demand in Korea market

edges.evidence: Stage 4 monthly Granger CSI->Korea lag 4, p=0.0229 (sign-corrected lens)

edges.causal_evidence: VARX bidirectional null, monthly Granger bidirectional null, mediation bidirectional × 2 transformations null, lagged cointegration bidirectional null

operational_use.interference_evidence: Prophet -9.01% / SARIMAX -10.94% RMSE degradation when Global lagged regressor added to Korea forecast (DM p<0.001, lag 11w)

narrative.primary: CSI directly and independently drives both Korea and Global search demand. The Global-Korea correlation reflects shape similarity (DTW lead ~10w, sign-corrected) without causal mediation (5-dimension orthogonal null × 11 tests). Global signal serves as monitoring leading indicator only — not as predictive feature for the Korea forecast model.
narrative.framing_description: BDC operational role preserved (Korea ↔ Global translator), direction reversed (Global precedes Korea). Common drivers (CSI + unobserved macro factors) act on both regions; Global registers shape changes earlier due to demographic concentration, faster fashion-cycle propagation, or HQ marketing-trigger precedence.

operational_distinction.monitoring: Global trend MA (4w) displayed as Korea demand directional reference in BDC dashboard right panel
operational_distinction.predictive: Korea Prophet forecast uses CSI exogenous only — Global lagged signal explicitly excluded due to documented forecast degradation
